The bridge at the crossing collapsed, no one's sure why
A whole caravan went missing on the north road
The neighboring settlement has been turning travelers away
Refugees from the eastern villages started arriving with nothing but the clothes on their backs
The garrison at the border outpost packed up and marched south without explanation
A courier came through saying the mountain pass is blocked by a rockslide
The village downriver put up a palisade wall where they never had one before
A pilgrim warned that the old road through the valley isn't safe after dark anymore
The lord of the nearest keep has been calling in debts and mustering workers
Travelers say there's a new camp on the trade road, and the people there aren't friendly
A messenger rode through at full gallop heading west and didn't stop for water
The settlement across the lake went dark three nights running, no fires or lanterns visible
A group of miners came down from the highlands saying the tunnels have been sealed
The ferry service on the wide river has been suspended with no word on when it resumes
A wandering priest says he was turned away from two towns before he reached here
Soldiers passed through headed north, wouldn't say where they were going or why
The trading post at the fork has been abandoned, shelves still stocked
Word is the harvest failed in the lowlands and they'll be looking to buy from anyone selling
A mapmaker came through and said half his landmarks don't match what's on the ground anymore
The coastal settlements sent word asking for able bodies, but the letter didn't say what for
A rider from the east said the watchtower there has been garrisoned for the first time in years
The road through the pass was cleared, and then blocked again within the week: nobody is saying by whom
A family arrived from the south saying their entire village packed up and left, but they don't agree on where everyone went
Travelers report a new toll gate on the highway that wasn't there last month and nobody seems to know who set it up
A courier's horse was found wandering with the saddlebags still sealed, but no sign of the courier
The monastery on the ridge sent a runner asking for supplies, which they've never done before
A caravan master says the oasis settlement has run dry and the people there are moving on
Word came that the fortified town to the north has closed its gates to all but residents
A trader from the coast says the harbor is silting up and the larger boats can't dock anymore
A group of laborers came through looking for work, said they were dismissed from a construction project that was cancelled overnight
A woman traveling alone said the inn at the crossroads burned down and no one is rebuilding
Soldiers from a foreign garrison were seen on the wrong side of the border, and they weren't in a hurry to leave
A messenger arrived with a letter sealed in black wax, delivered it to the council, and rode out without rest
The settlement upriver has started charging a fee to anyone passing through, even on foot
Word from the eastern road is that the waymarkers have been torn down and nobody has replaced them
A band of pilgrims changed their route to avoid a stretch of road and wouldn't explain what spooked them
Refugees from two different villages arrived telling the same story: they were told to leave, not asked
A scout returned and said the abandoned outpost has smoke rising from it again
The ferry operator on the lower river says the current has changed and something upstream is diverting the flow
A messenger from the capital passed through with an escort of six, which is four more than usual
Travelers report that the old stone road has a section that's sinking into the ground
A trader says the settlement at the crossroads has doubled in size since last season and nobody expected it
Word is the mining camp in the hills sent everyone home and posted guards at the entrance
A wandering healer says the sickness in the lowland towns is spreading faster than anyone's treating it
A courier relay station was found empty, the horses still in the stable and the fire still warm
The village on the other side of the forest has stopped sending runners, and their last message was weeks ago
A carter says the bridge downstream has been barricaded and the people on the far side won't explain why
Travelers from the north say the nights there have been colder than anyone remembers, even for the season
A tax assessor arrived from a jurisdiction nobody here falls under and started asking about resources
The road south has been washed out in three places and the repair crews haven't come
A merchant's wife said she heard the council of the nearest city declared a state of emergency, but no details followed
A group of refugees said they were turned away from two settlements before anyone would take them in
A rider came through warning that the river downstream flooded its banks and the low road is impassable
The outpost on the western approach is flying a flag nobody recognizes
Word from the coast is that the fishing fleet came back early and the captains held a closed meeting
A scholar traveling through said the library in the city has restricted access to certain records without explanation
Travelers report that a new settlement has sprung up in the contested borderlands, and it's growing fast
A caravan that was expected from the south arrived from the west, having detoured for reasons the drivers wouldn't discuss
The watchtower on the eastern road has a new garrison, but they're not from any organization the locals recognize
A family arrived saying they left because the well in their village went foul and nobody could find the cause
Word came that the council of the nearest town voted to arm the civilian population, though the reason wasn't included
A trader from the mountains says the pass is open but the people living near it have all moved to lower ground
A messenger relay horse arrived riderless at the next station, the message tube still sealed
Travelers say the road crews on the main highway stopped working and nobody is replacing them
A diplomat traveling with guards passed through and asked pointed questions about the local garrison's strength
Word from the river settlements is that boat traffic is being inspected by people who aren't the usual customs agents
A peddler says the town three days south has started building a second wall inside the first
Travelers report hearing horns from the direction of the old fort, which has been abandoned for a generation
A runner arrived from the western villages saying they need grain and they're willing to pay above market
The keep to the north sent a detachment through here without stopping, and they were moving fast
Word is the old trade route through the marshes is being reopened, but nobody will say by whose authority
A party of surveyors came through measuring the road and making notes, and they wore no insignia
Travelers from the coast say a ship arrived in port from waters no one recognized, and the crew didn't speak any local language
A courier was stopped at the border and his dispatches were confiscated, which has the mail system in disarray
The settlement at the river junction has posted a bounty, but the notice was vague about what the threat actually is
A traveling tinker says the three villages he usually visits on his circuit have all changed in ways he can't quite describe
Word from the highlands is that the seasonal herders didn't come down this year and nobody went to check on them
A merchant returning from the east says the road is lined with abandoned carts and nobody seems to be recovering them
Soldiers from the regional garrison passed through requisitioning horses and didn't offer the usual vouchers
A wandering scholar says the archives in the next settlement over were sealed by order of someone he couldn't identify
Travelers report that the settlement on the far side of the lake has started building watchtowers on every approach
A family moved here from two days' ride away and says the town they left was emptying out, one household at a time
Word is the coastal lookouts reported lights at sea where no ships should be
A courier says the next relay station posted armed guards and is turning away anyone without official business
The merchant convoy that usually arrives monthly is now arriving under escort and the goods cost more each time
Travelers from the south say a bridge that has stood for generations developed a crack that grows wider each week
A healer coming from the northern settlements says they've started quarantining arrivals, no exceptions
Word came that the old mine road has been cleared and widened, but no one filed a claim to justify the work
A patrol from the neighboring territory crossed into our jurisdiction and apologized, saying their maps were wrong: but the maps haven't changed
Travelers say the farming villages east of the river have all planted the same crop this season, which they've never coordinated before
A caravan guard deserted and came here looking for work, says he won't go back on that route for any amount of pay
Word is the religious order at the monastery recalled all their traveling members without explanation
A trader says the town at the crossroads has started stockpiling water in barrels, even though their river hasn't dropped
Travelers report finding the remains of a camp on the main road: recently abandoned, large enough for fifty people
A messenger arrived soaked and exhausted saying the dam upstream is showing cracks, but nobody seems to be reacting
The settlement across the valley started lighting signal fires at night, but no one here knows their meaning
A wandering musician says three different settlements offered him payment to carry a message, each addressed to a different recipient
Word from the border is that the checkpoint has been doubled in staff and everyone is being documented on entry
A family of craftspeople arrived saying they were bought out of their shop by someone who didn't want the business, just the building
Travelers say there's a stretch of road where the mile markers are all wrong: the distances don't add up
A rider passed through and told the innkeeper not to expect the next courier for a while, but didn't say why
Word is the old garrison to the south was reactivated last month and has been receiving supply wagons nightly
A merchant says a town he's been trading with for years didn't recognize his credentials on his last visit
Travelers report that the abandoned fort on the hilltop has lights in the windows at night
A woman arriving from the coast says the fishermen there found something in their nets that frightened them badly enough to stop fishing
Word from the river towns is that the ferry operators are all telling travelers to cross on foot at the shallows instead
A scout says the tree line on the northern ridge has moved: not a figure of speech, the actual forest boundary is different
Travelers from the west say a new road is being cut through the old forest without any announcement or posted authority
A courier says the signal relay between here and the capital has gone silent at one particular station and nobody can reach it
Word is the town on the far side of the pass has started turning away merchants who can't prove where their goods came from
A trader arriving from the south says the road is fine, the people are fine, everything is normal: and he looked terrified saying it
A group of builders arrived looking for work and said the project they came from was abandoned mid-construction, no reason given
Travelers report a column of smoke visible from the old ruins to the east, thick and persistent, not a campfire
A wandering clerk says the census records in the last three settlements he visited are all missing the same year
Word from the farmlands is that the irrigation canals are being diverted and the farmers downstream weren't consulted
A merchant caravan arrived short three wagons and the master said they were left behind at the pass and he'd rather not discuss it
Travelers say the old waystation on the mountain road has been reopened by people who don't answer to any settlement
A healer says the sickness she's been treating in the northern towns follows the trade road exactly: every settlement on the route is affected, none off it
Word is the harbor master at the coast has started refusing entry to certain ships and won't publish the criteria
A peddler coming from the east says two settlements he used to visit are now one: the smaller was absorbed and the residents seem unhappy about it
Travelers report that a stretch of the old highway has been repaved, expertly, and no one seems to know who did the work or when
A courier says the regional authority sent sealed orders to every settlement in the district, all delivered the same day
Word from the mountain villages is that snowfall came a full month early and the passes are already closing
A returning merchant says the market in the western city has been shut down for sanitation, which has never happened in living memory
A family arriving from the lowlands says the flood plain is being settled by newcomers nobody has seen before
Travelers report that the old border markers have been replaced with new ones that enclose a larger area
A runner from the coast arrived asking if we've seen a ship's crew come through on foot, which suggests the ship didn't make port
Word is the guild hall in the nearest city has been commandeered for a purpose the guild members weren't told about
A wandering storyteller says three different settlements commissioned him to tell the same story about their founding, but the stories don't match
Travelers from the north say the river there has changed color: not muddy, not polluted, just a different shade, as if the source changed
A carter says the road between here and the coast has new ruts in it from something much heavier than any ordinary wagon
Word came from the eastern settlements that their council meetings are now closed to the public for the first time in anyone's memory
A merchant arriving from the south says a new authority has posted decrees in a settlement that previously governed itself
Travelers report that the well-known landmark on the western road is gone: not ruined, not collapsed, just not there anymore
A messenger says the communication between settlements has slowed because the relay riders are being diverted to carry military dispatches instead
Word from the farming communities is that someone is buying harvests in advance at guaranteed prices, which sounds generous until you realize it means no surplus will reach the open market
A scholar passing through says three separate settlements have each claimed to have found old documents granting them control of the same stretch of land
Travelers say a new ford has been established downstream where the river widened, but the current there is deceptive and two carts have already been lost
A tinker coming from the north says three settlements along his route have all replaced their elders in the same month
Word from the river towns is that the fishermen are finding things in their nets that aren't fish and aren't debris: they won't say more
A pilgrim says the shrine on the eastern road has been defaced and nobody in the nearest settlement will acknowledge it
Travelers report that the old cemetery on the hilltop has fresh flowers on every grave, but no one in the area claims to have placed them
A carter says the road between the two northern settlements has a stretch where horses refuse to move faster than a walk
Word is the settlement on the far side of the marshes has started keeping their gates closed during the day as well as at night
A merchant passing through says the bridge at the southern crossing is guarded by people in uniform, but the uniform doesn't match any local authority
Travelers report that a hamlet they visited last season is now twice its size with construction everywhere, though nobody knows where the funding comes from
A wandering healer says the villages in the lowlands are all reporting the same symptoms in their livestock, but nobody's found a cause
Word came that the lighthouse on the coast went dark for three nights and the shipping losses haven't been tallied yet
A messenger says the capital dispatched riders in every direction the same morning and none of them carried the same message
Travelers from the west say a new road has been cut through land that was disputed for generations, and the dispute seems to have been settled overnight
A family arriving from the farming settlements says the soil there has changed color since last season and the crops grow differently
Word is the town at the river junction has started charging tolls on foot traffic for the first time in its history
A courier says the relay stations along the main road have all changed their flags to the same color, but nobody issued the order
Travelers report that the abandoned keep on the southern road has smoke from its kitchens and laundry on the line
A peddler coming through says two settlements that used to trade with each other have stopped all contact and neither will explain why
Word from the highland villages is that the sheep have been wandering to the same valley at night, all of them, from every flock
A trader says the market in the western city has been taken over by a single merchant house and the smaller vendors have been pushed out
Travelers report that the stone bridge over the deep gorge is humming, and the sound gets louder at night
A runner from the border settlements says a line of fires was visible on the far side of the frontier, more than any settlement would need
Word is the academy in the nearest city has suspended classes and the scholars were told to catalog their records as a matter of urgency
A carter says the waystations along the eastern highway are being renovated, but the laborers won't say who hired them or what standard they're building to
Travelers from the south say the water table has risen and basements that have been dry for decades are flooding
A courier says the town three days west has declared a harvest festival, but there's no harvest to celebrate: the fields are bare
Word from the mountain settlements is that the springs have stopped flowing and the snowmelt isn't reaching the valleys
A merchant arriving from the coast says the port authority has started requiring written declarations of intent from anyone who buys passage on a ship
Travelers report that a stretch of the old forest has gone quiet: no bird calls, no insects, just wind through the branches
A scholar passing through says three different libraries in the region have all lost their copies of the same manuscript
Word is the nearest garrison has been putting its soldiers through double drills and the quartermaster has been buying food as if for twice the number
A wandering priest says he's been asked to perform the same blessing in every settlement he's visited: protection against something none of them will name
Travelers from the north say the aurora was visible for three nights running, far south of where it should be seen
A family says the town they left has started rationing water, though the river flows strong and the wells are full
Word from the eastern road is that the milestones have all been marked with a new symbol painted in red, one that nobody can identify
A trade envoy says the annual summit between the regional settlements has been moved forward by two months and the agenda hasn't been shared
Travelers report that a crater appeared in a farmer's field east of here, perfectly round and deeper than a man is tall
A courier says the relay system has been instructed to prioritize certain sealed documents over all other traffic, and the couriers don't know the contents
Word is the old road through the Dead Wood has been reopened and people are using it again, despite the stories
A merchant from the south says the tax rate changed three times in a single season and nobody can keep track of what's owed
Travelers say the village at the base of the mountain has started posting guards on the trails leading up, though the trails have always been open
A wandering craftsman says every settlement he's visited in the last month has asked him the same question about reinforcing doors and shutters
Word from the far settlements is that the night sky has been different: unfamiliar patterns, or familiar patterns in the wrong positions
A rider says the old signal network between the border outposts has been reactivated after years of disuse, and the first signal was an alert
Travelers report that a settlement they passed through had every window shuttered in the middle of the day, and the people in the streets moved quickly and spoke quietly
A woman arriving from the lowlands says the insects there have changed: different species in the fields, different sounds at night
Word is the coastal watch has been doubled and the boats are being inspected twice: once at departure and once at arrival
A carter says he met a column of refugees on the main road who didn't know where they were going, only that they couldn't stay where they were
Travelers from the east say the old trade road that nobody used has more traffic on it now than the main highway
A messenger says the town at the crossroads has started requiring oaths of loyalty from new residents, which it has never done before
Word came from the northern settlements that three different councils have all passed the same law on the same day, though they claim not to have coordinated
A healer passing through says she's seen the same illness in four settlements that have no contact with each other, which shouldn't be possible if it spreads the usual way
Travelers report that the forest canopy over the old road has grown so thick that the road is in darkness at midday
A peddler says the settlement at the river bend has started building underground: cellars deeper than any cellar needs to be
Word is the old prison on the island in the lake has lights in it again, and boats have been going back and forth at night
A merchant says the currency from the eastern settlements has been devalued and the exchange rates are in chaos
Travelers say a messenger arrived at the nearest town riding a horse that was foaming and wild-eyed, delivered a single word to the council, and collapsed
A wandering scholar says the boundary markers between territories are being quietly moved, a few paces at a time, and nobody is officially responsible
Word from the farmlands is that the harvest predictions are wrong: the almanac says abundance, but the fields tell a different story
A traveler says the settlement he passed through yesterday morning was bustling and alive: by the time he looked back from the hilltop, it was dark and silent
Travelers report that the road crews have started building a detour around a section of the main highway, but the section they're avoiding looks perfectly fine
A carter says the toll station at the southern crossing now requires travelers to state their destination in writing before passing
Word from the highland settlements is that a fissure opened in the pastureland and the livestock won't graze near it
